commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fb7e1d8bb1ac481d2702e495cc136fb81b738a7f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch suppresses the parent doctor `groupAllowFrom` warning when every account has populated `groupAllowFrom`, adds scanner tests for that shape, and includes a small unrelated memory-core lint cleanup.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +58. Total +85 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main scans the parent channel record independently, and current policy/runtime source shows `allowFrom` can be the effective group allowlist for fallback-capable channels.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair on a maintainer-modifiable branch: match effective group allowlist semantics and add one fallback regression test.

target.title: #92684 fix(doctor): skip empty parent groupAllowFrom warning when all accounts override
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` warns about an empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` even when every child account has its own populated `groupAllowFrom`. The runtime resolves `account ?? parent`, so the parent's empty list is never read — the warning is a false positive.

**Root cause:** `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings()` checks the parent channel config independently of its child accounts. When the parent's `groupPolicy` is `"allowlist"` but `groupAllowFrom` is empty, it warns — without checking whether every account under it already supplies its own list.

**Fix:** Before emitting the parent-level group-allowlist warning, check whether **all** child accounts have their own populated `groupAllowFrom`. If they do, skip the parent warning (the parent scope is just a fallback that's never reached). DM-policy warnings at the parent scope are preserved.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor` false-positive warning when top-level `groupAllowFrom` is empty but every account overrides it with its own populated list.

**Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112, Node.js v24.13.1 via tsx, openclaw workspace importing the production `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` module.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
$ npx tsx repro-92684.ts

=== Test 1: All accounts override parent groupAllowFrom (no false warning) ===
Warnings: []
No false warning: PASS

=== Test 2: Some accounts lack groupAllowFrom (should still warn) ===
Warnings: ["- channels.signal.groupPolicy is ..."]
Has parent warning: PASS

=== Test 3: DM policy warnings preserved ===
Warnings: ["- channels.signal.dmPolicy is ..."]
DM warning preserved: PASS
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
Test 1 — all accounts override → no false warning:          PASS
Test 2 — some accounts lack → parent warning preserved:     PASS
Test 3 — DM warnings untouched by the change:               PASS
```

**Observed result after fix:** Parent-level group-allowlist warning is suppressed when every child account carries its own populated `groupAllowFrom`. DM-policy warnings at the parent scope continue to fire. When even one account lacks its own list, the parent warning is preserved.

**What was not tested:** Integration with specific channel plugins (signal, telegram, discord, etc.). The fix operates at the shared `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` layer before plugin-specific hooks fire, so it applies uniformly.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts` — 5 passed
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-policy.test.ts` — 4 passed
- [x] Regression: empty parent + all accounts override → no warning
- [x] Regression: empty parent + some accounts lack → warning preserved
- [x] Regression: DM warnings preserved at parent scope
target.author: pandah97

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
